units.subject: English Language and Literature
subtopics: Case-based factual passage with visual input (200-250 words) (10 marks)

units.subject: English Language and Literature
units.unit_name: Section B: Writing Skills and Grammar
key_concepts: Accuracy in spelling, punctuation and grammar

units.subject: English Language and Literature
subtopics: Short Answer Type Questions from BEEHIVE (40-50 words)
Short Answer Type Questions from MOMENTS (40-50 words)
Long Answer Type Question from BEEHIVE (100-120 words)

competencies.title: Oral and Written Communication Proficiency
outcomes: Build greater confidence and proficiency in oral and written communication
Make appropriate usage of English language both written and oral
Communicate in various social settings and express agreement and disagreement with logic

competencies.title: Critical Reflection and Inquiry
outcomes: Develop the ability and knowledge required to engage in independent reflection and inquiry
Equip learners with essential language skills to question and articulate their point of view
Arrive at conclusion through discussion and debate

competencies.title: Literary Appreciation and Cultural Sensitivity
outcomes: Develop sensitivity to, and appreciation of world literature representing varieties of English and cultures
Read poems effectively with proper rhythm and intonation and understand literary devices
Develop curiosity and creativity through extensive reading of literature from different time periods

competencies.title: Information Access and Self-Learning
outcomes: Enable the learner to access knowledge and information through reference skills
Facilitate self-learning to enable them to become independent learners
Review, organise and edit their own work and work done by peers

competencies.title: Functional Language Application
outcomes: Give a brief oral description of events/incidents of topical interest
Retell the contents of authentic audio texts
Participate in conversations and discussions on topics of mutual interest
Respond in writing to business letters, official communications, emails etc.
Write without prior preparation on a given topic (article, speech, or debate)

competencies.title: Multimodal Literacy and Transcoding
outcomes: Read and identify main points of texts like scripts of audio-video interviews
Write a summary of short lectures by making/taking notes
Transcode information from a graph/chart to a description/report
Write dialogues, short stories or reports based on visual cues
